---
title: "BN combined with PCA"
author: "Jirka Vomlel"
date: "2026-03-25"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

## 

Gaussian BN learning on PCA-transformed variable groups
with combined BIC (BN BIC + PPCA log-likelihood penalty)

Reference: Tipping M.E. & Bishop C.M. (1999). Probabilistic Principal Component Analysis. JRSS-B, 61: 611-622. https://doi.org/10.1111/1467-9868.00196
Required package: bnlearn. Install with: install.packages("bnlearn")

```{r}
library(bnlearn)
```

1. PPCA log-likelihood at ML estimates for a single group Model: 
x = W z + mu + eps,  z ~ N(0, I_k),  eps ~ N(0, sigma^2 I_p) => x ~ N(mu, W W^T + sigma^2 I_p)
  
ML estimates (Tipping & Bishop 1999):
    sigma^2_hat = mean of discarded eigenvalues of the ML covariance
    W_ML        = U_k (Lambda_k - sigma^2 I)^{1/2} R  (R arbitrary rotation)

Log-likelihood at ML estimates:
    L = -(n/2) [ p log(2 pi) + sum_{j=1}^k log(lambda_j) + (p-k) log(sigma^2_hat) + p ]

Arguments:
  X_group : n x p_j matrix for this group (raw, un-centered is fine)
  k       : number of retained PCs (must satisfy 1 <= k < p_j)

Returns: scalar log-likelihood value

```{r}
ppca_loglik <- function(X_group, k) {
  n <- nrow(X_group)
  p <- ncol(X_group)
 
  if (k < 1 || k >= p)
    stop(sprintf("k must satisfy 1 <= k < p. Got k=%d, p=%d.", k, p))
 
  # ML covariance eigenvalues = sample eigenvalues scaled by (n-1)/n
  # (prcomp returns sdev based on division by n-1)
  pca_res    <- prcomp(X_group, center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE)
  eigenvalues <- (pca_res$sdev^2) * (n - 1) / n
 
  # ML noise variance: average of discarded eigenvalues
  sigma2_hat <- mean(eigenvalues[(k + 1):p])
 
  if (sigma2_hat <= .Machine$double.eps) {
    warning(sprintf(
      "Group with p=%d, k=%d: noise variance is (near) zero; PPCA likelihood undefined.",
      p, k))
    return(NA_real_)
  }
 
  # Log-likelihood at ML estimates
  loglik <- -(n / 2) * (
    p * log(2 * pi) +
    sum(log(eigenvalues[1:k])) +
    (p - k) * log(sigma2_hat) +
    p
  )
 
  return(loglik)
}

ppca_discarded_loglik <- function(X_group, k) {
  n <- nrow(X_group)
  p <- ncol(X_group)

  if (k < 1 || k >= p)
    stop(sprintf("k must satisfy 1 <= k < p. Got k=%d, p=%d.", k, p))

  pca_res    <- prcomp(X_group, center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE)
  eigenvalues <- (pca_res$sdev^2) * (n - 1) / n

  sigma2_hat <- mean(eigenvalues[(k + 1):p])

  if (sigma2_hat <= .Machine$double.eps) {
    warning(sprintf("Group p=%d, k=%d: sigma2 near zero.", p, k))
    return(NA_real_)
  }

  -(n * (p - k) / 2) * (log(2 * pi * sigma2_hat) + 1)
}

```

2. Number of free parameters in PPCA for a single group
  - Loading matrix W: k*p entries, minus k*(k-1)/2 for rotational
    indeterminacy (the non-identifiability of W up to orthogonal rotation)
  - Noise variance sigma^2: 1 parameter
  - Group means mu: p parameters

Arguments:
  p : number of variables in the group
  k : number of retained PCs

Returns: integer parameter count

```{r}
ppca_nparams <- function(p, k) {
  k * p - k * (k - 1) / 2 + 1 + p
}
```

3. Main function: learn BN on PC scores, compute combined BIC

Arguments:
  data   : n x P data frame or matrix (all observed variables)
  groups : list of length g, each element is an integer vector of column
           indices belonging to that group; groups must be disjoint
  n_pcs  : integer vector of length g; n_pcs[j] = number of PCs for group j
           must satisfy 1 <= n_pcs[j] < length(groups[[j]])

Returns: named list with
  $bn_structure   bnlearn bn object (learned DAG)
  $bn_fit         bnlearn bn.fit object (fitted Gaussian BN)
  $pc_data        data frame of all PC scores used as BN variables
  $bn_loglik      log-likelihood of the BN on pc_data
  $bn_bic_score   BIC score of BN from bnlearn (= loglik - log(n)/2 * d_BN)
  $bn_nparams     number of free parameters in the BN
  $ppca_logliks   vector of PPCA log-likelihoods per group
  $ppca_nparams   vector of PPCA parameter counts per group
  $total_loglik   bn_loglik + sum(ppca_logliks)
  $total_nparams  bn_nparams + sum(ppca_nparams)
  $bic_combined   the combined BIC criterion (higher = better)

```{r}
learn_bn_pca_bic <- function(data, groups, n_pcs) {

  data <- as.matrix(data)
  n    <- nrow(data)
  g    <- length(groups)

  if (length(n_pcs) != g)
    stop("Length of n_pcs must equal number of groups.")
  for (j in seq_len(g)) {
    p_j <- length(groups[[j]])
    if (n_pcs[j] < 1 || n_pcs[j] >= p_j)
      stop(sprintf("Group %d: n_pcs=%d must satisfy 1 <= n_pcs < %d.", j, n_pcs[j], p_j))
  }

  pc_list          <- vector("list", g)
  ppca_ll_vals     <- numeric(g)
  ppca_nparam_vals <- integer(g)

  for (j in seq_len(g)) {
    X_j <- data[, groups[[j]], drop = FALSE]
    k_j <- n_pcs[j]
    p_j <- ncol(X_j)

    pca_j    <- prcomp(X_j, center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE)
    scores_j <- pca_j$x[, 1:k_j, drop = FALSE]
    colnames(scores_j) <- paste0("G", j, "_PC", seq_len(k_j))

    pc_list[[j]]        <- scores_j
    ppca_ll_vals[j] <- ppca_discarded_loglik(X_j, k_j)   
    ppca_nparam_vals[j] <- ppca_nparams(p_j, k_j)             
  }

  pc_data <- as.data.frame(do.call(cbind, pc_list))
  bn_dag  <- hc(pc_data, score = "bic-g")
  bn_fit  <- bn.fit(bn_dag, pc_data)

  bn_ll      <- as.numeric(logLik(bn_fit, data = pc_data))
  bn_bic_val <- score(bn_dag, data = pc_data, type = "bic-g")
  bn_nparams <- round(2 * (bn_ll - bn_bic_val) / log(n))

  total_ll      <- bn_ll + sum(ppca_ll_vals)
  total_nparams <- bn_nparams + sum(ppca_nparam_vals)
  bic_combined  <- total_ll - (log(n) / 2) * total_nparams

  list(
    bn_structure  = bn_dag,
    bn_fit        = bn_fit,
    pc_data       = pc_data,
    bn_loglik     = bn_ll,
    bn_bic_score  = bn_bic_val,
    bn_nparams    = bn_nparams,
    ppca_logliks  = ppca_ll_vals,
    ppca_nparams  = ppca_nparam_vals,
    total_loglik  = total_ll,
    total_nparams = total_nparams,
    bic_combined  = bic_combined
  )
}
```

 4. Example usage

```{r}
set.seed(42)
n <- 200

# Simulate 10 variables with some correlation structure
Sigma <- matrix(0.4, nrow = 10, ncol = 10)
diag(Sigma) <- 1
L     <- chol(Sigma)
X_sim <- matrix(rnorm(n * 10), nrow = n) %*% L
colnames(X_sim) <- paste0("V", 1:10)

# Define 3 groups: {V1..V4}, {V5..V7}, {V8..V10}
groups <- list(1:4, 5:7, 8:10)
n_pcs  <- c(2, 2, 2)   # retain 2 PCs per group

result <- learn_bn_pca_bic(X_sim, groups, n_pcs)

cat("=== Learned BN structure ===\n")
print(result$bn_structure)

cat("\n=== PPCA log-likelihoods per group ===\n")
for (j in seq_along(groups)) {
  cat(sprintf("  Group %d: loglik = %.3f,  nparams = %d\n",
              j, result$ppca_logliks[j], result$ppca_nparams[j]))
}

cat("\n=== BN component ===\n")
cat(sprintf("  BN log-likelihood : %.3f\n", result$bn_loglik))
cat(sprintf("  BN BIC score      : %.3f\n", result$bn_bic_score))
cat(sprintf("  BN nparams        : %d\n",   result$bn_nparams))

cat("\n=== Combined BIC ===\n")
cat(sprintf("  Total log-likelihood : %.3f\n", result$total_loglik))
cat(sprintf("  Total nparams        : %d\n",   result$total_nparams))
cat(sprintf("  Combined BIC         : %.3f\n", result$bic_combined))
```

5. Granularity search: compare combined BIC across different n_pcs choices

```{r}

cat("\n=== Granularity search over number of PCs per group ===\n")

# Define candidate PC counts per group (must be < group size)
candidates <- list(
  G1 = 1:3,   # group 1 has 4 variables -> k in {1,2,3}
  G2 = 1:2,   # group 2 has 3 variables -> k in {1,2}
  G3 = 1:2    # group 3 has 3 variables -> k in {1,2}
)

grid <- expand.grid(candidates)
results_grid <- vector("list", nrow(grid))

for (i in seq_len(nrow(grid))) {
  k_vec <- as.integer(grid[i, ])
  res_i <- learn_bn_pca_bic(X_sim, groups, k_vec)
  results_grid[[i]] <- data.frame(
    G1_npc       = k_vec[1],
    G2_npc       = k_vec[2],
    G3_npc       = k_vec[3],
    bic_combined = res_i$bic_combined
  )
}

grid_df <- do.call(rbind, results_grid)
grid_df <- grid_df[order(-grid_df$bic_combined), ]
cat("\nTop 5 granularity configurations (higher BIC_combined = better):\n")
print(head(grid_df, 5), row.names = FALSE)

best_idx <- which.max(grid_df$bic_combined)
cat(sprintf("\nBest configuration: G1=%d PC, G2=%d PC, G3=%d PC  (BIC_combined=%.3f)\n",
            grid_df$G1_npc[best_idx],
            grid_df$G2_npc[best_idx],
            grid_df$G3_npc[best_idx],
            grid_df$bic_combined[best_idx]))
```
